text.headline: Juan Santiago - Professor Chronicle
text.text: An academic journey from undergraduate studies through distinguished professorship, spanning research excellence, innovation, mentorship, and national recognition.

text.headline: B.S. in Mechanical Engineering, University of Florida
text.text: Undergraduate foundation in mechanical engineering at University of Florida.

text.headline: M.S. in Mechanical Engineering, UIUC
text.text: Master's degree from University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign, beginning graduate formation.

text.headline: Ph.D. in Mechanical Engineering, UIUC
text.text: Doctoral research at University of Illinois establishing foundation for microfluidics career.

text.headline: Ford Foundation Postdoctoral Fellow, Beckman Institute UIUC
text.text: Postdoctoral research as Ford Foundation Fellow and Research Scientist at UIUC's Beckman Institute for Advanced Science and Technology.

text.headline: Joined Stanford University faculty
text.text: Faculty appointment in Mechanical Engineering at Stanford University, establishing the Stanford Microfluidics Laboratory.

text.headline: Frederick Emmons Terman Fellow Award
text.text: Terman Fellowship awarded upon joining Stanford, recognizing exceptional promise.

text.headline: Collegiate Inventors Award
text.text: National Inventors Hall of Fame Collegiate Inventors Award recognizing early innovative work.

text.headline: NSF CAREER Award
text.text: National Science Foundation CAREER Award, the NSF's most prestigious award for early-career faculty.

text.headline: Presidential Early Career Award (PECASE)
text.text: Presidential Early Career Award for Scientists and Engineers, the highest federal honor for early-career researchers.

text.headline: Fellow, American Physical Society
text.text: Elected Fellow of the American Physical Society for contributions to fluid mechanics and transport phenomena.

text.headline: Fellow, American Society of Mechanical Engineering
text.text: Elected Fellow of ASME for exceptional engineering contributions and leadership.

text.headline: Fellow, American Institute for Medical and Biological Engineering
text.text: Elected Fellow of AIMBE for contributions to biomedical engineering and medical applications.

text.headline: Vice Chair, Mechanical Engineering Department
text.text: Appointed Vice Chair of Stanford's Mechanical Engineering Department, formalizing institutional leadership role.

text.headline: AES Lifetime Achievement Award
text.text: Analytical and Environmental Sciences Lifetime Achievement Award recognizing career-long contributions.

text.headline: Member, National Academy of Inventors
text.text: Elected to NAI for exceptional innovation record: 60 issued patents, 27 currently licensed.

text.headline: Member, American Academy of Arts and Sciences
text.text: Elected to AAAS, one of the nation's oldest and most prestigious honorary societies.

text.headline: Member, National Academy of Engineering
text.text: Elected to NAE, the highest professional distinction for engineers, recognizing transformative contributions to microfluidics and transport phenomena.

text.headline: Editor-in-Chief, Flow (Cambridge University Press)
text.text: Serves as Editor-in-Chief of Cambridge journal Flow, shaping discourse in fluid mechanics internationally.

text.headline: Research Impact: 220+ journal papers, h-index 91
text.text: Sustained research productivity with 220+ journal papers, 250+ conference papers, h-index of 91, and 1500+ annual citations.

text.headline: Innovation Record: 60 patents, 27 licensed
text.text: 60 issued patents with 27 currently licensed, spanning microfluidics, diagnostics, and water treatment technologies.

text.headline: Mentorship Legacy: 32 PhD students and postdocs
text.text: Trained 32 PhD students and postdocs; 24 now faculty at major universities, 8 in corporate labs, 4 founded startups.
